sections.paragraph:
καυσόω (‘to suffer from καῦσος [burning heat]’; Ptolem., Apotelesm. 1, 4, 4 Boll-B.; PHolm 25, 27) pass. be consumed by heat, burn up (Diosc. 2, 134 W.; Antyllus in Oribas. 9, 13, 1; Galen, CMG V 9, 1 p. 264, 13; Philumen. p. 26, 21 of fever) στοιχεῖα καυσούμενα λυθήσεται the elements will be destroyed by burning 2 Pt 3:10; cp. vs. 12.—On the destruction of the world by fire s. Rtzst., Weltuntergangsvorstellungen 1924; s. also FOlivier, 2 Pt 3:10: Religio 11, ’35, 481–89; Hengel, Judaism II 128 n. 552.—DELG s.v. καίω 4. TW.

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

Boll Boll = FB., Aus der Offenb. Johannis—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

PHolm PHolm = Papyrus Graecus Holmiensis—List 4

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

CMG CMG = Corpus Medicorum Graecorum—List 5

Philumen Philumen , II a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Rtzst. Rtzst. = RReitzenstein—List 6

Hengel, Judaism Hengel, Judaism = MH., Judaism and Hellenism—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 536
